name: quickcode
description: Windsurf-style autonomous coding agent powered by local OpenAI-compatible LLMs.

commands.title: QuickCode: Show Background Explorer Activity Log

quickcode.llm.baseURL.description: OpenAI-compatible base URL (Ollama / LM Studio / vLLM).

quickcode.llm.apiKey.description: API key (some local servers ignore this; leave default for Ollama).

quickcode.llm.model.description: Model name as exposed by your local server.

quickcode.llm.contextWindow.description: Total context window of the model in tokens.

quickcode.llm.allowSelfSignedCerts.description: Skip TLS certificate verification when talking to the configured baseURL. Enable only for trusted self-hosted endpoints with self-signed certificates.

quickcode.llm.endpoints.description: List of OpenAI-compatible endpoints. Each entry is a remote server (baseURL + apiKey). Models for each endpoint can be fetched live via /v1/models from the chat panel's model picker, or supplemented manually in the 'models' field below. The chat panel shows endpoints grouped in the picker.

name.description: Display name shown in the model picker.

apiKey.description: API key (optional for most local servers).

allowSelfSignedCerts.description: Skip TLS certificate verification for this endpoint's baseURL.

models.description: Manually-curated model ids for this endpoint. The picker shows these in addition to anything fetched from /v1/models.

quickcode.llm.activeEndpoint.description: Name of the currently active endpoint from quickcode.llm.endpoints. Set automatically when picking a model in the chat panel.

quickcode.llm.activeModel.description: Currently active model id under the active endpoint. Set automatically when picking a model in the chat panel.

quickcode.llm.profiles.description: [Deprecated] Legacy LLM profiles (endpoint+model combined). Still read for backward compatibility — each profile is migrated into a quickcode.llm.endpoints entry on the fly. Prefer quickcode.llm.endpoints for new setups.

quickcode.llm.activeProfile.description: [Deprecated] Legacy active-profile name. Used only as a fallback when quickcode.llm.activeEndpoint is empty.

quickcode.git.autoCheckpoint.description: Before applying any QuickCode edit, snapshot the working tree to refs/quickcode/checkpoints/<timestamp> so the change can be rolled back later.

quickcode.agent.maxIterations.description: Maximum tool-calling iterations per user request.

quickcode.agent.autoContinueOnLength.description: When the LLM stops mid-turn because of the output token limit (finish_reason=length), automatically prompt it to continue instead of ending the turn.

quickcode.agent.maxAutoContinues.description: Maximum number of consecutive auto-continues before giving up. Resets whenever the model successfully calls a tool.

quickcode.lsp.maxWaitMs.description: Maximum time to wait for a language server to be ready.

quickcode.context.outlineBaseDepth.description: Recursion depth used when generating the workspace outline injected into the agent's system prompt. Higher = more files visible but more tokens spent.

quickcode.context.outlineSrcDepth.description: Recursion depth applied to source-like directories (src, lib, app, packages, ...) so the model can see deeper into them.

quickcode.context.outlineMaxBytes.description: Soft cap (in characters) on the workspace outline embedded in the system prompt. Outlines exceeding this are truncated and the agent is told to drill in via workspace_outline.

quickcode.context.outlineExtraExcludes.description: Additional directory basenames to exclude from the workspace outline (in addition to defaults like node_modules, dist, out, .git).

quickcode.background.enabled.description: Enable the background Code Explorer. When the IDE is idle (no chat run in progress and no recent edits), QuickCode will pick one source file at a time, read it, and write a per-file summary, suspected-bug log, and auto-generated unit tests for uncertain points to the workspace's output directory. Pauses automatically while the user is typing or while a chat request is being processed.

quickcode.background.idleThresholdMs.description: Idle gap (ms) after the latest real text edit before a background analysis cycle starts. Cursor/editor/window changes only pause an already-running background job; they do not keep postponing the next cycle.

quickcode.background.minIntervalMs.description: Minimum gap (ms) between successive background cycles, even when the user has been idle the whole time.

quickcode.background.filesPerCycle.description: How many files to analyse per cycle. Each file = one LLM call.

quickcode.background.maxConcurrentTopics.description: How many investigation topics to run in parallel within a single cycle. Each topic uses its own AgentLoop and therefore its own LLM streams. Increase to use the full cycle window when your endpoint allows multiple concurrent requests; lower to 1 for strictly sequential execution.

quickcode.background.includeExtensions.description: File extensions (no leading dot) the background explorer is allowed to read and analyse.

quickcode.background.maxFileBytes.description: Skip files larger than this many bytes. Keeps each LLM call from blowing past the model's context window.

quickcode.background.outputDir.description: Workspace-relative directory where the explorer writes docs/, bugs.md, tests/, and state.json. Created on first run; safe to delete.

quickcode.background.endpoint.description: Endpoint name (from quickcode.llm.endpoints) the background explorer should use. Empty = inherit the chat panel's currently active endpoint.

quickcode.background.model.description: Model id the background explorer should use under its endpoint. Empty = the endpoint's first known model, falling back to chat's active model. Lets you pair the background loop with a smaller / cheaper model than the one you chat with.

quickcode.background.temperature.description: Sampling temperature for background analysis calls.

quickcode.background.contextWindow.description: Override the context window (tokens) used to size background prompts. 0 = inherit from the resolved endpoint.

quickcode.background.perFileTimeoutMs.description: Cancel a single-file analysis if it has not finished within this many milliseconds.

quickcode.background.runGeneratedTests.description: When enabled, the background explorer will spawn the appropriate test runner (vitest/jest for TS/JS via npx, pytest for Python) on each generated unit test file and record pass/fail/skip results to verifications.md and a per-test .result.md. Off by default — running auto-generated code touches your shell and dependencies; review the test files first before enabling on untrusted projects.

quickcode.background.testRunTimeoutMs.description: Per-test execution timeout (ms). The runner is killed and recorded as 'error: timed out' if it exceeds this duration.
